# Broader Impacts

## Goal

A broader impacts section should show how the research creates value beyond the narrow technical result and how that value will be pursued intentionally.

## Strong Broader Impacts Patterns

- Integrate student training into the research workflow
- Partner with existing outreach or workforce programs
- Release reusable data, software, or teaching materials
- Include measurable participation or access goals
- Tie dissemination to real audiences rather than a generic public

## Practical Categories

### Education and Training

- Undergraduate research participation
- Graduate and postdoctoral mentoring
- Course modules tied to project outputs
- Workshops or tutorials

### Broadening Participation

- Recruitment through existing institutional programs
- Partnerships with minority-serving institutions
- Paid research experiences for underrepresented students
- Mentoring structures with named owners

### Community or Societal Benefit

- Public data resources
- Decision-support tools
- Policy engagement
- Workforce development
- Environmental or health impact pathways

## Make It Credible

For each activity, specify:

- Who owns it
- Who the audience is
- What will be delivered
- When it will happen
- How success will be measured

## Weak Patterns to Avoid

- Generic claims about inspiring students
- Outreach with no delivery mechanism
- Activities unrelated to the project
- No evaluation plan
- Reusing boilerplate that could fit any proposal

## Review Checklist

- Activities are specific
- The timeline is plausible
- Outcomes can be measured
- Institutional partners or resources are named
- The section feels integrated with the science rather than appended
